sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.85 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: A subset of a group is said to be if it can be obtained from finite subsets by finitely many rational operations, that is, taking union, product, and the submonoid generated by a set. Conjecture\/: every finitely generated solvable group in which all rational subsets form a Boolean algebra is virtually abelian. This is known to be true if the group is metabelian, or polycyclic, or of finite rank (...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
